campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary rule that was put in place urgently, and approved plans to apply for grants to build a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth scholarship program
Council extends temporary interim ordinance 2326
Approval to pursue grant funding for Hamilton Avenue bike bridge over Highway 17
Final feasibility study accepted for Highway 17 bicycle overcrossing project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or law. After listening to residents' comments, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the normal first reading step to speed up the process, with plans to finalize and vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next council meeting
Expedited approval process being used

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to finalize their 2026 weed removal program, establish new rules for how council meetings will run, and update housing policies around affordable units. The council also approved raising two flags at city hall—one for Pride Month and one for Juneteenth—and received a transportation presentation from the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority.
keyTopics: Completion of 2026 weed abatement program across the city
New affordable housing rule allowing Below Market Rate units to be transferred under certain conditions
Updated council procedures and meeting norms
Two new flag raisings planned at city hall (Progress Pride and Juneteenth)
Regional transportation planning updates from VTA

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with construction projects and professional services. The council also voted to join a statewide insurance program (CJPIA) to help manage the town's insurance costs and coverage.
keyTopics: Construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Professional services agreement with Dudek
Joined California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance rates
Multiple contract amendments with various service providers

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review progress on programs helping kids and teens in our community, including youth empowerment initiatives and summer safety programs. They also discussed how our libraries are doing and reviewed the city's code enforcement activities for the past year.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and summer programs to keep teens safe and engaged
Updates on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan
Library facilities improvements and customer service
Code Enforcement activity and violations handled

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included both in-person and virtual attendance options. Residents were able to participate remotely via Zoom video conference or phone call to ask questions and share their views on city matters.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Hybrid meeting format allowing in-person and online attendance

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved meeting notes from their previous gathering in March.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission meeting held
Personal stories shared by Commissioners 5, 6, and 7
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community programs and initiatives. The council heard presentations about a food-related program called Budget and Bites, learned about a youth organization called Unmuted Tabs, and discussed summer job opportunities for young people in the city.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth organization
City of Cupertino Summer Jobs program
Approval of previous meeting minutes from March 11, 2026
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 using a hybrid format, meaning residents could attend in person at City Hall or join remotely via Zoom. The city provided multiple ways for the public to watch and participate, including video conferencing and phone dial-in options.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and remote via Zoom)
Remote participation options: Zoom video link and phone dial-in number provided
Written public comments can be submitted through eComment tab on City Council Agenda page
Meeting accessibility for all residents regardless of location or ability to attend in person

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative and financial matters, including joining a tax board partnership with the county, deciding on fee waivers for a community educational organization, awarding a construction contract for a city project, and reviewing housing/community development plans. Council members also requested new items to be added to the city's work agenda.
keyTopics: Joining Franchise Tax Board partnership with county
Fee waiver decision for Milpitas Community Educational organization
Construction project contract approval and funding
Updates to housing and community development plans (2025-2030)
New projects and priorities proposed by individual council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss the city's gas utility finances and rates for the next fiscal year. The main decision involved approving a financial forecast and adjusting gas rates for three different customer categories: regular homes, apartment buildings/small businesses, and large commercial users.
keyTopics: Gas utility budget and financial reserves for 2027
Gas rate changes for residential customers (G-1 schedule)
Gas rate changes for apartments and commercial buildings (G-2 schedule)
Gas rate changes for large commercial users (G-3 schedule)
